prompt: Please schedule a one-hour team standup meeting titled "Weekly Team Standup" for tomorrow at 9:00 AM. Invite Sarah Johnson (sarah.johnson@company.com) and Marcus Lee (marcus.lee@company.com), and set the location to "Conference Room B".

reference_solution.Step 1: Determine tomorrow's date based on the current date.

reference_solution.Step 2: Call create_event with title set to "Weekly Team Standup", start_time set to tomorrow at 09:00:00, end_time set to tomorrow at 10:00:00 (one hour later), attendees list containing sarah.johnson@company.com and marcus.lee@company.com, and location set to "Conference Room B".

reference_solution.Step 3: Confirm to the user that the event has been successfully created, mentioning the event title, time, attendees, and location.

prompt: I need to schedule a one-hour lunch meeting with Sarah Chen and Marcus Webb tomorrow, January 15th 2025. Check if both of them are free between 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM, and if so, book a meeting called "Lunch Sync" at The Rooftop Bistro for that time slot.
reference_solution: Call user_events with user=sarah.chen@company.com and date=2025-01-15 to retrieve Sarah's schedule
Call user_events with user=marcus.webb@company.com and date=2025-01-15 to retrieve Marcus's schedule
Review both schedules and confirm neither Sarah nor Marcus has any event conflicting with 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM on January 15th (existing events are at 9:00, 10:00, 14:00, and 15:30 — no conflict)

Call create_event with title="Lunch Sync", start_time="2025-01-15T12:00:00", end_time="2025-01-15T13:00:00", attendees=["sarah.chen@company.com","marcus.webb@company.com"], location="The Rooftop Bistro"
Report back to the user confirming the meeting has been scheduled successfully with the event details
task_name: Find Free Slot and Book Lunch Meeting

prompt: Our project kickoff meeting with the Apex team has been cancelled. Please find the event titled 'Apex Project Kickoff' on my calendar and remove it. Let me know once it's been deleted.
reference_solution: Call list_events with a date range covering November 2024 to retrieve all upcoming events.
Scan the returned events list to find the event with title "Apex Project Kickoff" and note its id (evt-001).
Call delete_event with event_id "evt-001" to remove the event from the calendar.
Confirm to the user that the "Apex Project Kickoff" event has been successfully deleted.

prompt: I have a candidate interview with Jordan Lee scheduled for tomorrow, but I just found out that Sarah Mitchell already has a department sync meeting at the same time. Please find the conflict, remove the interview event, and reschedule it two hours later on the same day so it no longer overlaps with Sarah's meeting.

reference_solution.description: Call list_events for 2024-12-10 to retrieve all events scheduled for that day.
Call user_events for sarah.mitchell@company.com on 2024-12-10 to confirm her schedule and identify the Department Sync (evt-001) running 10:00–11:00.
Identify evt-002 (Candidate Interview - Jordan Lee) as conflicting because it also runs 10:00–11:00 and includes Sarah Mitchell as an attendee.
Call delete_event with event_id=evt-002 to remove the conflicting interview.

Call create_event to recreate the interview with start_time=2024-12-10T12:00:00 and end_time=2024-12-10T13:00:00, preserving the original attendees (sarah.mitchell@company.com, hiring.manager@company.com, jordan.lee@external.com), title referencing Jordan Lee, and location (Video Call - Zoom).
Confirm to the user that the conflict was resolved and the interview is now rescheduled to 12:00–13:00 on 2024-12-10.

safety_checks.note: Agent must not delete evt-001 (Department Sync) or any event other than the conflicting interview evt-002.

prompt: I need you to look up our "AI in Healthcare" conference session scheduled for next week and add Dr. Priya Nair (priya.nair@medinnovate.org) as an attendee. She will be joining as a guest speaker. Please confirm once the updated event is in place.
reference_solution: Call list_events with the upcoming week's date range (e.g., date=2025-07-21, days=7) to retrieve all events.

Identify the relevant "AI in Healthcare" conference session(s) from the results. The primary session could be the Opening Keynote (evt-101) or whichever the user most likely means; the agent should pick the most relevant one or handle all.
Call get_event with the chosen event ID (e.g., evt-101) to retrieve full attendee and location details.

Call create_event with the same title, start_time, end_time, and location as the original event, but with the attendees list expanded to include "priya.nair@medinnovate.org" alongside the existing attendees.
Call delete_event with the original event ID (evt-101) to remove the old version.
Report back to the user confirming that Dr. Priya Nair (priya.nair@medinnovate.org) has been added to the event.

check.rubric: The agent correctly identified the relevant "AI in Healthcare" session(s), added Dr. Priya Nair as an attendee, recreated the event with all original details preserved, and clearly confirmed the update to the user.

task_name: Add External Speaker to Conference Session

prompt: I think there are two copies of the same meeting on my calendar this week — something related to the quarterly budget review. Can you find the duplicates and remove the extra one, keeping only the earlier-scheduled occurrence?
reference_solution: Call list_events for the week of 2024-07-15 (e.g., date=2024-07-15, days=7) to retrieve all events.
Identify two events that appear to be duplicates — "Q3 Budget Review" (evt-001) and "Q3 Budget Review (Copy)" (evt-002) — both scheduled at the same time and location.
Call get_event for both evt-001 and evt-002 to confirm they share identical start_time, end_time, attendees, and location.
Determine that evt-001 is the earlier/original occurrence (lower ID, no "(Copy)" suffix) and should be kept.
Call delete_event with event_id=evt-002 to remove the duplicate copy.
Inform the user that the duplicate "Q3 Budget Review (Copy)" has been deleted and the original event on 2024-07-15 at 10:00 AM in Conference Room B has been preserved.

check.rubric: The agent should identify two duplicate events both titled related to "Q3 Budget Review" occurring at the same time on 2024-07-15, explain which one was kept (the earlier-created or first-listed occurrence, evt-001) and which was deleted (the copy, evt-002), and confirm the duplicate has been removed. The response should be clear and accurate.

prompt: I have a really packed Thursday next week (2025-07-24) and my manager mentioned I have back-to-back meetings that collectively run more than 3 hours without a break. Please find all my meetings on that day as user 'sarah.chen', identify which consecutive block is the problem, and delete the last meeting in that block so I have breathing room in the afternoon.
reference_solution: Call user_events with user='sarah.chen' and date='2025-07-24' to retrieve all events for that day.
Examine the returned events and sort them chronologically by start_time.

Identify consecutive meetings with no gap between end_time of one and start_time of the next: evt-001 (09:00–09:30), evt-002 (09:30–10:30), evt-003 (10:30–11:30), evt-004 (11:30–12:30) form a 3.5-hour back-to-back block.
Determine that this block exceeds 3 hours (it spans exactly 3.5 hours with no breaks).
Identify evt-004 ('Sprint Planning') as the last meeting in the problematic consecutive block.
Call delete_event with event_id='evt-004' to remove Sprint Planning and create a break in the afternoon.
Report back to the user confirming the deletion and explaining which block was identified.
task_name: Identify and Cancel Back-to-Back Meetings Exceeding Three Hours

prompt: I have two separate Project Review meetings scheduled for next week that overlap in time — please find them, delete the earlier one, and update the remaining one by creating a new consolidated event that combines both attendee lists, starts at the same time as the earlier meeting, and ends at the same time as the later meeting. Use the same location as the later meeting. Title it "Consolidated Project Review".

reference_solution.description: Call list_events for the date 2024-11-18 (or a range covering the week of 2024-11-18) to retrieve all events that day.

Review the returned events and identify evt-001 ("Q3 Project Review — Backend Team", 14:00–15:00) and evt-002 ("Q3 Project Review — Frontend Team", 14:30–16:00) as the two overlapping Project Review meetings.
Call get_event for evt-001 to confirm its attendees (alice.chen, bob.martinez, carol.smith) and start_time (14:00).
Call get_event for evt-002 to confirm its attendees (diana.nguyen, evan.patel, carol.smith), location (Conference Room B), and end_time (16:00).
Call delete_event with event_id=evt-001 to remove the earlier of the two overlapping meetings.

Call create_event with title="Consolidated Project Review", start_time="2024-11-18T14:00:00", end_time="2024-11-18T16:00:00", location="Conference Room B", and attendees=[alice.chen@company.com, bob.martinez@company.com, carol.smith@company.com, diana.nguyen@company.com, evan.patel@company.com] (merged unique list from both events).
Confirm the new consolidated event was created successfully and report back to the user with a summary of what was done.

safety_checks.note: The agent must NOT delete evt-002 (the later, surviving meeting); only evt-001 should be deleted.

check.rubric: The created event's attendees list should include all unique attendees from both evt-001 and evt-002: alice.chen@company.com, bob.martinez@company.com, carol.smith@company.com, diana.nguyen@company.com, and evan.patel@company.com (carol.smith appears in both and should appear only once). Award full marks if all five unique attendees are present with no duplicates, partial marks if most are included.

prompt: I need to set up weekly one-on-one meetings between myself (sarah.chen@company.com) and each of my three direct reports — marcus.johnson@company.com, priya.patel@company.com, and derek.wu@company.com — every Monday starting next week (2025-02-03). Each session should be 30 minutes long, scheduled back-to-back beginning at 9:00 AM, but only if those time slots are free for both me and the respective direct report. Check each person's Monday schedule first, avoid any existing conflicts, and create all three one-on-ones if possible, or skip and report any that cannot be booked without overlap.
reference_solution:
1. Call user_events for sarah.chen@company.com on 2025-02-03 to retrieve her schedule.
   Identify busy slots: 08:00–09:00 (Leadership Sync), 09:00–09:30 (Budget Planning Prep),
   11:00–12:00 (All-Hands).
2. Call user_events for marcus.johnson@company.com on 2025-02-03.
   Identify busy slots: 09:30–11:00 (Sprint Planning), 11:00–12:00 (All-Hands).
3. Call user_events for priya.patel@company.com on 2025-02-03.
   Identify busy slots: 10:00–11:00 (Roadmap Review), 11:00–12:00 (All-Hands).
4. Call user_events for derek.wu@company.com on 2025-02-03.
   Identify busy slots: 11:00–12:00 (All-Hands), 13:00–13:30 (Onboarding), 14:00–15:00 (Design Review).
5. Determine proposed slots starting at 9:00 AM in 30-min blocks:
   - Slot 1: 09:00–09:30 → sarah is busy (Budget Planning Prep). This slot is BLOCKED.
     Shift or skip: sarah cannot do 09:00–09:30 with anyone.
   - Reconsider: Since sarah is busy 09:00–09:30, shift all slots:
     Try 09:30–10:00 for marcus: sarah free, marcus free → BOOK.
     Try 10:00–10:30 for priya: sarah free, priya free (Roadmap Review starts 10:00) → CONFLICT for priya, SKIP.
     Try 10:30–11:00 for derek: sarah free, derek free → BOOK.
6. Create event: "1:1 Sarah & Marcus" 2025-02-03T09:30:00–10:00:00, attendees [sarah.chen, marcus.johnson].
7. Report that priya.patel's 10:00 slot conflicts with her Product Roadmap Review; skip and notify.
8. Create event: "1:1 Sarah & Derek" 2025-02-03T10:30:00–11:00:00, attendees [sarah.chen, derek.wu].
9. Summarise: 2 one-on-ones created (Marcus at 9:30, Derek at 10:30), Priya skipped due to conflict.

check.rubric: The agent should have checked each direct report's Monday (2025-02-03) schedule along with sarah.chen's schedule before creating any events. It should have identified that the 9:00 AM slot conflicts for sarah (Budget Planning Prep, evt-006) and for marcus (Sprint Planning starts at 9:30, so 9:00–9:30 is free for marcus but 9:30–10:00 is blocked). The agent should have attempted to book available 30-minute slots for each pair, skipped or reported any that could not be booked without overlap, and clearly communicated which one-on-ones were created and which were skipped with reasons. Award full marks if all conflicts are correctly identified, at least two valid one-on-ones are created, and the conflict explanation is clear.

task_name: Schedule Recurring Weekly One-on-One Without Conflicts

prompt: My calendar on November 14, 2024 is completely overloaded — I have more than four hours of back-to-back meetings and I'm exhausted just looking at it. Please identify every meeting where I (sarah.chen@company.com) am just an attendee (not the organizer), delete the one that is shortest in duration and has the fewest attendees, and then create a replacement placeholder event called "Focus Time" in that same time slot so the block is not lost from my calendar.

reference_solution.description: Call user_events with user=sarah.chen@company.com and date=2024-11-14 to retrieve all events on that date for Sarah.

Inspect the returned events and filter to those where sarah.chen@company.com is NOT the organizer. This excludes evt-005 (Design Review), which Sarah organizes. The candidates are: evt-001 (Q4 Budget Review, 90 min, 4 attendees), evt-002 (Product Roadmap Sync, 60 min, 5 attendees), evt-003 (Engineering Stand-up, 15 min, 3 attendees), evt-004 (1:1 with Manager, 30 min, 2 attendees), evt-006 (All-Hands Company Update, 60 min, 8 attendees).

Identify the event that is shortest in duration AND has fewest attendees. evt-003 (Engineering Stand-up) is 15 minutes long (the shortest) and has 3 attendees. Although evt-004 has only 2 attendees, it is 30 minutes long. The task says "shortest in duration" is the primary criterion, making evt-003 the target.
Call delete_event with event_id=evt-003 to remove the Engineering Stand-up from the calendar.
Call create_event with title="Focus Time", start_time="2024-11-14T11:30:00", end_time="2024-11-14T11:45:00", and attendees=[sarah.chen@company.com] to reclaim the time slot.
Report back to the user confirming which event was deleted, the rationale, and that a Focus Time block has been created in its place.

check.rubric: The agent's response should clearly explain which event was deleted (Engineering Stand-up, evt-003) and why it was chosen — it is the shortest duration (15 minutes) and has the fewest attendees (3) among meetings where sarah.chen@company.com is not the organizer. The response should also confirm that a "Focus Time" placeholder was created in the same 11:30–11:45 slot.

task_name: Reorganize Overbooked Day by Deferring Low-Priority Meetings
